title: Order of Operations
essential_question: What is the order of operations and why does it matter?
strategies: Then multiply and divide from left to right.
Then add and subtract from left to right.

learn.title: What Is the Order of Operations?
learn.content: When an expression has more than one operation, you need rules to know which to do first. Without rules, different people get different answers! The order of operations gives everyone the same result.

steps: 1. Parentheses — solve what is inside ( ) first
2. Multiply and Divide — left to right
3. Add and Subtract — left to right

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Multiply Before You Add
learn.content: Look at 3 + 4 × 2. If you add first: 3 + 4 = 7, then 7 × 2 = 14 (wrong!). If you multiply first: 4 × 2 = 8, then 3 + 8 = 11 (correct!). Always multiply or divide before you add or subtract.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Parentheses Change the Answer
learn.content: Adding parentheses can change the result completely. Compare: 2 + 3 × 4 = 2 + 12 = 14 (no parentheses — multiply first). But (2 + 3) × 4 = 5 × 4 = 20 (parentheses first — different answer!).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Division Before Subtraction
learn.content: Division also comes before addition and subtraction. In 20 − 15 ÷ 3: divide first → 15 ÷ 3 = 5. Then subtract → 20 − 5 = 15. If you subtract first you get the wrong answer.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Label Each Step
learn.content: When an expression is complex, label every step: Step 1 — Parentheses, Step 2 — × or ÷, Step 3 — + or −. Example: 2 + 3 × (8 − 2). Step 1: 8 − 2 = 6. Step 2: 3 × 6 = 18. Step 3: 2 + 18 = 20.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 연산 순서 루틴 — 3단계 우선순위
learn.content: 연산 순서 (3학년판):   1단계 괄호 ( ) — 가장 먼저.   2단계 ×와 ÷ — 왼쪽에서 오른쪽으로.   3단계 +와 − — 왼쪽에서 오른쪽으로. 예: 3 + 2 × 4 → 2×4=8 먼저 → 3+8=11. 예: (3 + 2) × 4 → 괄호 5 먼저 → 5×4=20. 흔한 실수 (M02): 왼쪽부터 차례로만 풀어 3+2=5 → 5×4=20 (잘못 — 정답 11). 팁: 머릿속으로 ×÷ 부분에 동그라미 치기.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 괄호의 힘 — 답을 완전히 바꿈
learn.content: 괄호 ( )가 있으면 그 안을 무조건 먼저. 예: 3 + 2 × 4 = 11 (괄호 없음 → 곱셈 먼저). 예: (3 + 2) × 4 = 20 (괄호 → 덧셈 먼저). 같은 숫자·기호인데 답이 다름! 팁: 괄호 표시 = '이걸 먼저 해라'는 명령. 흔한 실수: 괄호 무시하고 표준 순서만 적용.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic G — Order of Operations Without Exponents
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 단계별 검산 — 한 줄씩 답 옮기기
learn.content: 복잡한 식은 한 단계씩 다음 줄로 옮겨 적기. 예: 3 × (4 + 5) − 7   단계 1) 괄호: 4+5 = 9 → 식: 3 × 9 − 7.   단계 2) 곱셈: 3×9 = 27 → 식: 27 − 7.   단계 3) 뺄셈: 27 − 7 = 20. 🔍 검증: 각 단계가 정확한지 별도 검산. 흔한 실수 (M02): 머릿속만 의지해 한 단계 빠뜨리거나 순서 뒤바뀜 — 종이에 적기.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.11 'Order of Operations' pp.305-308

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 802 − 367.
practice_r2.explanation: 802−367: 2−7 borrow → 12−7=5; tens 9−6=3 (after lending across zero); hundreds 7−3=4. Result: 435.

practice_r3.explanation: Multiply first: 3 × 2 = 6. Then 5 + 6 = 11. Leo is correct.
hints: Apply order of operations: what operation comes before adding?

feedback.correct: Correct! 3 × 2 = 6 first, then 5 + 6 = 11. Leo is right.
feedback.incorrect: Multiply first: 3 × 2 = 6. Then 5 + 6 = 11. Leo is correct, not Sara.

practice_r3.question: Sara says 5 + 3 × 2 = 16. Leo says it equals 11. Who is correct?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 3 × 2 = 6 first, then 5 + 6 = 11. Leo is right.

hints: Evaluate all four choices using order of operations.

feedback.correct: Right! A = 30 is the greatest. B=22, C=18, D=13.
feedback.incorrect: Calculate each: A=(2+4)×5=30, B=2+4×5=22, C=2×9=18, D=8+5=13. A is greatest.

practice_r3.question: Which has the greatest value?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! A = 30 is the greatest. B=22, C=18, D=13.

hints: When there are no parentheses, do × before +.

feedback.correct: Correct! 2×3=6 first, then 4+6=10 — different from Sam's parentheses version!
feedback.incorrect: Without parentheses: multiply first → 2×3=6, then add → 4+6=10.

practice_r3.question: Sam solves (4 + 2) × 3 = 18 with parentheses. What is the value of the same expression WITHOUT parentheses: 4 + 2 × 3 = ?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 2×3=6 first, then 4+6=10 — different from Sam's parentheses version!
